Gradient-Boosted Trees (integer-coded)
Per-feature token granularity (PLR transfers across token granularities)
Unified per-feature affine token for mixed types
Tunable per-field embedding dimension
Per-feature non-shared embedding parameter blowup
Target-aware bin construction leakage
O(k^2) attention cost in feature count
Naive scalar*vector numerical embedding
Categorical embedding table grows linearly with cardinality
Supervised embedding transfer leakage across models
Concat-then-MLP implicit interaction does not scale
A Time Series is Worth 64 Words (PatchTST)
AutoInt: Automatic Feature Interaction via Self-Attentive Networks
Why do tree-based models still outperform deep learning on tabular data?
Temporal Patch Tokenization (subseries patching)
Channel-Independence (shared-weight univariate)
Masked Patch Pretraining (MAE-style)
Point-wise Temporal Token (per-timestep)
Multi-Head Self-Attention Interacting Layer (AutoInt)
Per-Field Embedding Tokenizer
Residual Connection
Field-Shared Attention (params independent of feature count)
Factorization Machines (FM)
Instance Normalization (RevIN-style)
Per-feature embedding breaks rotation invariance
Field-shared interaction layer: zero attention params added per feature
Patching collapses long per-feature time axis to control sequence length
Channel-independence deliberately drops cross-feature interaction
AutoInt attention compute is O(M^2) in feature count
AutoInt numerical embedding is rank-1 scalar*vector (weak)
Rotation-invariant MLPs destroy meaningful per-feature basis
Grinsztajn benchmark excludes high-cardinality / missing / temporal
TabPFN v2: tabular foundation model (Nature 2025)
Temporal Fusion Transformer (TFT)
DCN V2: Improved Deep & Cross Network
Per-Cell Two-Way (Feature+Sample) Attention Tokenization (TabPFN v2)
Prior-Data Fitted Network: synthetic-SCM in-context pretraining
Variable Selection Network (VSN, instance-wise soft feature gating)
Full-Matrix Hadamard Cross Layer (DCN-V2)
Low-Rank Mixture-of-Experts Cross (DCN-Mix)
CrossNet Rank-1 Cross Layer (DCN-V1)
Feature-axis attention to mix many heterogeneous features (no O(features^2)-over-sequence cost)
Synthetic-SCM-prior pretrained tokenizer that generalizes across schemas
Variable selection as a learned per-feature soft-gating tokenizer over mixed types
Explicit interaction operator with cost in embedding-dim d, independent of field count M
'Foundation model' does NOT mean wide-table scale (v2 capped ~500 feat / ~10k samp)
Query-set coupling: predictions depend on batch composition (not row-independent)
Feature-count inflation: TFT's 370/440 are ENTITIES not features; VSN untested at 70+ features
Cross cost is O(d^2) in flattened input dim d; low-rank/MoE mandatory at width
Raw-scalar numeric input, no learned numeric embedding (weak numeric leg)
Headline AUC gain is small in absolute terms and recsys-only (CTR/LTR)
CARTE: Pretraining and Transfer for Tabular Learning
Recommender Systems with Generative Retrieval (TIGER / RQ-VAE Semantic IDs)
iTransformer: Inverted Transformers Are Effective for Time Series Forecasting
Column-Name LM Embedding as Relation (CARTE edge feature)
Graphlet Row Tokenization (row as star graph of value/colname pairs)
FastText String Embedding (open-vocabulary cell/colname encoder)
Contrastive Pretraining with Edge Dropout (YAGO graphlets, InfoNCE)
Open-vocabulary (column-name-embedding, value) tokenizer for cross-schema transfer
Scaling claim without 70+-feature evidence (CARTE: <=2048 rows, ~15-relation graphlets)
Weak numerical encoder (scalar*colname-vector, no PLR/periodic)
Benchmark composition string-heavy / high-cardinality (advantage shrinks on numeric tables)
RQ-VAE Semantic ID (residual-quantized hierarchical discrete codes)
VQ-VAE Quantization (codebook + commitment loss)
LSH Semantic ID (locality-sensitive-hashing codes)
Recsys generative-retrieval machinery imported wholesale (only RQ-VAE part transfers)
Variate-as-Token Inverted Embedding (whole series -> one token, attention over variates)
Feature-as-token over joint attention with shared per-token weights (scales to many features via subsampling)
Per-feature variate token unifies time-varying + static features into one joint-attention sequence
'Variates' are numeric series, not mixed numeric+categorical+static heterogeneity
iTransformer ties PatchTST on low-dimensional data; edge is only on many-correlated-variate sets
TabLLM: Few-shot Classification of Tabular Data with LLMs
TabICL: A Tabular Foundation Model for In-Context Learning on Large Data
TabICLv2: A better, faster, scalable, open tabular foundation model
Text Serialization of Rows (row->NL sentence + LLM subword tokenizer)
T-Few Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning ((IA)^3 scaling vectors)
Token-budget scaling wall (1024-token cap; <=30 columns; lossy truncation at 70+ features)
Shared Set-Transformer Column Embedder (ISAB hypernetwork emits per-cell W,B; no per-feature params)
Column-Collapse to Per-Row Token (CLS pool feature embeddings -> one row token before ICL)
RoPE over the Feature Axis (de-collapse identically-distributed features)
RoPE breaks column-permutation invariance (only restored by 32-shuffle ensemble; 32x inference tax)
Wide-table (>100 feat) behavior is inference-extrapolation, not the <=100-feature pretraining regime
Target-Aware Embedding (TAE: add EmbedTAE(y) into context-row feature embeddings)
Column-then-Row(-then-Dataset) Attention (TF_col -> TF_row CLS pool -> TF_icl)
QASSMax (scalable softmax attention variant)
Repeated Feature Grouping (each token bundles 3 circularly-shifted columns)
SAINT: Improved Neural Networks for Tabular Data via Row Attention and Contrastive Pre-Training
Intersample (Row) Attention (row-as-token, learned soft-kNN over the batch)
Contrastive CutMix+Mixup Self-Supervised Pretraining (SAINT)
Per-Feature Token Embedding (single ReLU continuous embedding + categorical token)
TabTransformer (contextual categorical embeddings; baseline architecture)
Intersample/row attention as a learned soft-kNN — transferable to scaling/interaction at width
Intersample attention is quadratic in (n*d): projection matrices (n*d)x(n*d) blow up with feature count
Row attention makes predictions batch-composition-dependent (non-i.i.d. per-row inference)
Encoding High-Cardinality String Categorical Variables
MinHash n-gram Encoder (stateless, vocabulary-free fixed-width string encoder)
Gamma-Poisson n-gram Matrix Factorization (interpretable latent categories)
Target Encoding (label-conditioned categorical encoding)
MinHash coordinates are not a metric space — raw codes not directly meaningful for attention/distance
TabArena: A Living Benchmark for Machine Learning on Tabular Data
TabNet: Attentive Interpretable Tabular Learning
T2G-Former: Organizing Tabular Features into Relation Graphs
GReaT: Language Models are Realistic Tabular Data Generators
Sparsemax Instance-Wise Feature Selection (TabNet attentive mask)
Masked Feature Reconstruction Pretraining (TabNet self-supervised)
Per-Feature Self-Attention Tokenization (FT-Transformer-style, contrast)
Linear-cost O(D) instance-wise feature gate (sparsemax) that scales in feature count
Tabular masked self-supervised pretraining (reconstruct masked features)
TabNet supervised wins likely on under-tuned baselines (not reproduced by later benchmarks)
TabNet impoverished input encoding (raw numerics + plain categorical embeddings, no PLR)
FR-Graph: Learned Sparse Feature Relation Graph (T2G-Former)
Graph Estimator (GE: data-adaptive + static-topology sparse interaction)
Dense Self-Attention over feature tokens (all-pairs, baseline)
FR-Graph imposes a sparse interaction PRIOR but compute stays O(M^2) (no FLOP saving)
T2G evaluated <=93 features, no temporal; static-topology-freeze training hack
Random Feature-Order Permutation (order-invariance training, GReaT)
Text Serialization 'COLUMN_NAME is VALUE' (subject-predicate-object clauses)
LLM Subword (BPE) Tokenization of serialized rows
CTGAN Conditional GAN (tabular synthesis baseline)
TVAE (tabular VAE synthesis baseline)
Order-invariant, arbitrary-conditioning tokenizer via random feature-order permutation
GReaT eval caps at 47 features; no 70+-feature evidence
Raw-digit numerics serialized as text, no numeric embedding (weak numeric leg)
Serial autoregressive sampling cost wall (generation-side, not an encoder)
TabReD: Analyzing Pitfalls and Filling the Gaps in Tabular Deep Learning Benchmarks
Random splits on temporally-ordered data inflate rankings (time-leakage)
Retrieval-augmented DL (TabR/ModernNCA) underperforms under temporal drift (hypothesis-level cause)
Per-numerical-feature (PLR) embeddings keep their benefit on wide (median-261-feature) drifting industrial tables
XTab: Cross-table Pretraining for Tabular Transformers
Federated Cross-Table Backbone Pretraining (FedAvg over shared transformer blocks)
Reconstruction SSL from Feature Corruption (SCARF-style marginal resampling)
Decouple per-table tokenizer from cross-table interaction backbone (transfer only the interaction prior)
Cross-table pretraining transfers the backbone, NOT the tokenizer (featurizer re-initialized per table)
LIFT: Language-Interfaced Fine-Tuning for Non-Language ML Tasks
Text-Serialization Fine-Tuning (row->NL sentence + ordinary autoregressive LM fine-tune)
LoRA (low-rank parameter-efficient fine-tuning)
LLM-serialization context wall + high-D regression collapse (raw-digit numerics, p>=50 fails)
AutoDis: An Embedding Learning Framework for Numerical Features in CTR Prediction
TabTransformer: Tabular Data Modeling Using Contextual Embeddings
TabM: Advancing Tabular Deep Learning with Parameter-Efficient Ensembling
Soft Discretization over Per-Field Meta-Embeddings (AutoDis: differentiable scorer + temperature softmax + weighted-average over a tiny shared codebook)
Hard Discretization Binning (EDD/LD/TD fixed bins; non-differentiable, SBD/DBS artefacts)
Per-Column Identifier Embedding (concatenated shared column id replaces positional encoding so attention knows the source field)
Contextual Categorical Embeddings (cross-column self-attention turns context-free per-column embeddings into contextual ones)
Generator-Free Per-Column Replaced-Token-Detection Pretraining (ELECTRA-style RTD for tabular; per-column binary heads, no auxiliary generator)
Parameter-Efficient Ensembling (TabM: k implicit submodels via shared weights + cheap per-submodel adapters; mean at inference)
BatchEnsemble Adapters (rank-1 multiplicative/additive per-submodel adapters around shared weights; the first multiplicative adapter R is load-bearing)
Per-column field-provenance token: give each feature token a learned column identifier (by concatenation) so attention can disambiguate which field a value came from
Flat-concat per-feature PLE/PLR embeddings into a linear-cost MLP (ensemble) backbone beats attention tokenizers on wide tables without per-token attention

Soft-discretization temperature is load-bearing: too high -> uniform mixing (loses resolution); too low -> argmax (degenerates to hard binning, reintroduces SBD/DBS). Global optimum varies wildly across datasets.
AutoDis 'scalable' = constant per-field parameter budget, NOT demonstrated many-feature / high-attention-cost scaling; do not read as a 70+-feature result.
TabTransformer numerical features bypass the Transformer (raw normalized concat, never attended) -> on numerical-heavy 70+ tables most input gets no contextualization; the gap FT-Transformer closes.

TabM default one-hot categoricals inflate input width / first-layer params linearly with total category count -> wrong primitive for high-cardinality 70+-feature tables; swap in learned/hashing/target encoding.

TabM is neither a tokenizer (borrows one-hot+quantile+PLE) nor an explicit feature-interaction/selection method (no cross/attention/gate); its only tie to the route is linear-cost flat-concat MLP scaling.
ExcelFormer: A Neural Network Surpassing GBDTs on Tabular Data
BatchEnsemble: An Alternative Approach to Efficient Ensemble and Lifelong Learning
TabR: Tabular Deep Learning Meets Nearest Neighbors in 2023
Semi-Permeable Attention (SPA: mutual-information mask lets low-importance features attend to high-importance ones but not vice-versa; noise-robustness inductive bias)
Feat-Mix / Hid-Mix (importance-weighted tabular Mixup data augmentation; mixes features/hidden units weighted by mutual-information feature importance)
CatBoost (ordered) Target Encoding (leakage-controlled target encoding used as ExcelFormer's categorical-to-numeric scalarizer)
Retrieval-Augmented Tabular Prediction (attend over retrieved training instances; an instance-axis alternative to per-feature tokenization)
Key-Only L2 Retrieval Attention (TabR: similarity over keys via L2 distance, value-as-difference; feature-count-invariant attention over the sample axis)
Noise-robust wide-feature attention: an importance-masked (semi-permeable) attention prior that protects high-importance features from noisy uninformative ones at large feature width
Tokenizer-agnostic cheap ensembling: rank-1 BatchEnsemble adapters wrap ANY backbone/tokenizer for near-free accuracy+calibration gains, parameters independent of feature count
Instance-axis retrieval is feature-count-invariant: attending over training rows (not features) sidesteps the O(k^2)-in-feature-count tokenizer cost on wide tables

ExcelFormer has no native categorical/temporal tokenizer: categoricals are CatBoost-target-encoded into scalars (lossy scalarization), no temporal handling -> SPA/Feat-Mix transfer but the tokenizer itself does not cover mixed types
SPA is a noise-robustness inductive bias, NOT a scaling mechanism: attention stays O(k^2) in feature count; static mutual-information mask computed once, untested above 54 features

BatchEnsemble rank-1 adapters are an efficient-ensemble approximation (not a full deep ensemble) and not a tokenizer; evidence is non-tabular (vision/lifelong), tabular transfer only inferred via TabM

Train/deploy retrieval leakage: retrieval-augmented tabular models can leak across the train/deploy boundary on temporal+static mixed data (which rows are retrievable at inference vs train differs); acute under temporal drift
Training Independent Subnetworks for Robust Prediction (MIMO)

MIMO is neither a tokenizer nor a feature-interaction method, and its 'free' ensembling gain is contingent on backbone over-parameterization (collapses on tightly-fit nets unless input-repetition rho is raised, which trades away diversity); 'for free' is prediction-time only (batch repetition raises training cost)
UniTabE: A Universal Pretraining Protocol for Tabular Foundation Model in Data Science
TabUnit Cell Key-Value Tokenization (each cell = (column-name embedding, data-type embedding, value tokens); schema-agnostic per-cell unit admitting any column count / incremental columns)

Data-Type Embedding + Fuse/Linking Gates (explicit numerical/categorical/textual type indicator gated into the column-name vector, then woven into every value token so flat-concat attention recovers field-value binding and type)
Multi-Cell Masking + Contrastive Pretraining (mask whole cells, auto-regressively regenerate; missing-value objective == downstream objective; plus row-block InfoNCE contrastive)

Explicit data-type embedding as the feature-typing primitive: a small type indicator (num/cat/text/missing), gated into each feature token, disambiguates feature types in one schema-agnostic tokenizer

UniTabE textualizes numerics: digit/character sub-word tokens through the shared text embedder, magnitude only implicitly recoverable via the type flag; no PLR/periodic/quantile magnitude-aware numerical embedding (the exact weakness the numerical-embeddings route fixes)

Per-cell multi-token expansion: one cell = (1 + q) tokens so a 70+-column table yields a sequence MUCH longer than the column count; O(L^2) attention in total token length, no linear-attention escape hatch ('scales to many features' = schema flexibility, NOT compute efficiency)

13B-Kaggle / 303-domain pretraining may share domains with the 7 public benchmarks (only the 12 Kaggle tasks are guaranteed held out), so the public-benchmark lead could be partly leakage; scratch model already hits 0.81 vs 0.83 finetuned (pretraining lift on public sets is modest, win is mostly TabUnit architecture)
Trompt: Towards a Better Deep Neural Network for Tabular Data

Prompt-Learning Importance Gate (Trompt: learned input-independent column embeddings + P soft-prompt vectors -> per-sample per-prompt softmax importance over columns via O(P x C) batched matmul, replacing O(C^2) self-attention; interpretable column-level importances)
Per-Feature Value Tokenizer (FT-Transformer-style: scalar->Dense for numerical, embedding lookup for categorical, one d-dim token per column)
Feature Self-Attention (all-pairs O(C^2) attention over feature tokens; the mechanism Trompt's prompt-importance gate replaces)

Trompt's Grinsztajn45 win is on a benchmark that filters out high-cardinality categoricals and missing values at moderate feature counts; 'narrows the gap to GBDT' is NOT evidence of 70+-heterogeneous (esp. high-card) scaling. Part-3 B x P x C x d expansion materializes a large activation tensor (P=128), so the O(P x C) framing hides a large constant on wide tables; supplementary FT/SAINT tables are un-tuned (not apples-to-apples)
Making Pre-trained Language Models Great on Tabular Prediction (TP-BERTa)
Arithmetic Feature Interaction Is Necessary for Deep Tabular Learning (AMFormer)
TimeXer: Empowering Transformers for Time Series Forecasting with Exogenous Variables
Relative Magnitude Tokenization (RMT: C4.5 target-aware per-feature bins -> shared, triplet-ordered magnitude-token vocabulary, embedding scaled by raw value)
Intra-Feature Attention (IFA: shared mini-self-attention fuses each feature's name+value sub-tokens into ONE token; sequence length = feature count)
Magnitude-aware numerical tokens: give an LM/transformer a target-aware, order-regularized shared bin-token vocabulary scaled by raw value (decouples name semantics from value magnitude)
Name+value fusion to one token: a shared mini-attention collapses each feature's column-name and value sub-tokens to a single feature token, making sequence length = feature count
TP-BERTa RMT bins are built by C4.5 LABEL-guided splits (target-aware); leakage risk unless bin boundaries are constructed out-of-fold (same hazard as PLEt target-aware binning)
TP-BERTa pretrained/validated at <=32 features (IFA makes sequence length = feature count but pretraining corpus capped low-dim); 70+-feature behavior is untested extrapolation
TP-BERTa's headline win is categorical/semantic-name-dominated; it loses on all-numerical tables, and the comparison uses default HPs vs tuned baselines (rank-only headline)
Multiplicative Attention in Log Space (AMFormer: attention over log(ReLU(X)+eps) then exp = learned weighted products, parallel to additive attention; from Neural Arithmetic Logic Units)
Prompt-Token Query Attention (AMFormer: learned prompt tokens are the attention queries, making attention cost linear in feature count N and stabilizing data-invariant interaction patterns)
Hard Top-k Attention (keep the k largest QK^T entries per row, mask the rest to -inf so they vanish after softmax; sparse interaction with k independent of N)

Explicit multiplicative feature interaction: a log-space attention + exp stream gives a per-feature transformer the ability to synthesize ratio/product features (BMI-style) that additive attention cannot
Endogenous Global Bridge Token (TimeXer: one learnable [CLS]-style token per target series that aggregates patches AND is the sole QUERY into exogenous variate tokens via 1xC cross-attention)
Role-Asymmetric Patch+Variate Tokenization (TimeXer: tokenize the target you care about at fine patch granularity, collapse each context feature to ONE coarse variate token)

TimeXer single-global-bridge-token bottleneck: funneling all exogenous info through ONE token loses to iTransformer joint attention on high-interaction many-variate sets (e.g. Traffic); numeric/temporal only, no categorical/static pathway
AMFormer multiplicative-stream instability: log(ReLU(X)+eps) zeroes/!undefined for non-positive inputs and is sensitive to eps; signed-log alternative untested; can destabilize training
AMFormer 'arithmetic interaction is necessary' necessity claim leans on synthetic monomial data; real-world breadth is weak (4 datasets, single run); re-validate on in-the-wild TabReD/TabArena
Time-Series Foundation Models
Recsys & Semantic-ID Tokenization (transfer)
Industrial & Production Feature Systems
Libraries & Reference Implementations
All dense features collapsed into one token (no per-numerical interaction)
Fixed [-15,+15] quantization range: overflow on spikes + precision loss
No built-in high-cardinality categorical compression
No learned missingness mask (NaN as index-0 only)
'Scales' conflates big embedding tables with many interacting feature tokens
Timestamp = calendar features, not patch/forecast temporal tokenization
Univariate only — no covariates, static attributes, or categoricals
Explicit pairwise dot-product feature interaction (DLRM)
Model-parallel embedding-table sharding
Regression-via-Classification (cross-entropy over value bins)
Scale + Uniform-Quantize Value Tokenization (Chronos)
stype -> StypeEncoder -> concatenate registry (PyTorch Frame)
Numeric value binning into a fixed codebook for an off-the-shelf transformer
Shared d-dim field-token space (one token per field)
stype/encoder registry as a feature-typing front-end for 70+ heterogeneous features
Chronos: Learning the Language of Time Series
Deep Learning Recommendation Model (DLRM)
PyTorch Frame: A Modular Framework for Multi-Modal Tabular Learning
Compositional Embeddings Using Complementary Partitions (Quotient-Remainder trick)
Quotient-Remainder Trick (two complementary index maps share an O(sqrt(|S|)) embedding budget)
Complementary Partition Embeddings (multiple complementary category partitions composed into one embedding)
Hashing Trick (fixed hash maps high-cardinality ids into a bounded embedding table; collisions tolerated)
Bound high-cardinality categorical embedding memory to O(sqrt(|S|)) via complementary-partition composition (no accuracy loss vs full table on recsys)
Compositional/QR embeddings are a categorical memory-compression trick only: they save memory, do NOT improve accuracy, and give no help for numerical features
Unified Training of Universal Time Series Forecasting Transformers (Moirai)
Any-Variate Attention (binary field-ID bias + RoPE; parameter-free, unbounded number of variates)
Multi-Patch-Size Projection Bank (per-frequency patch sizes {8,16,32,64,128} into one model)
Mixture-Distribution Output Head (4-component mixture for flexible predictive distributions)
Channel-as-Token (one variate/channel -> one token over which attention runs)
Any-variate attention is quadratic in (variates x sequence length): flattening all variates+patches into one sequence blows up at 70+ features x long histories
Moirai has no categorical or static-covariate pathway: variates are numeric series only; mixed numeric+categorical+static heterogeneity is unmodeled
Hash Embeddings for Efficient Word Representations
Hash Embedding (importance-weighted multi-hash into a shared component-vector pool; learnable collision repair)
With a single hash function (k=1) hash embeddings degenerate to the plain hashing trick: collided ids are indistinguishable; multi-hash importance weights are what repair collisions
Hashing/hash-embedding compression only pays off above a cardinality threshold: below it a plain learned embedding table is as good and simpler
TOTEM: TOkenized Time series EMbeddings for General Time Series Analysis
Better by Default: Strong Pre-Tuned MLPs and Boosted Trees on Tabular Data (RealMLP)
High-Cardinality Categorical & Feature-Encoding Practice in Winning Tabular / Production Pipelines
Channel-Independent Tokenization (flatten each channel into its own univariate token sequence sharing ONE codebook)
Fixed Uniform Value Binning (Chronos-style scale-then-uniform-quantize value tokens)
PBLD Periodic-Bias-Linear-Densenet Numerical Embeddings (per-feature cosine basis + original value -> R^4)
Learnable Diagonal Scaling Layer (per-feature scalar s_i before first linear; implicit feature-importance reweighting)
Meta-Tuned Default (TD) Hyperparameters (tune one config on a meta-train suite, freeze, ship no-HPO defaults)
Ordered Target Statistics (CatBoost: encode each example from random-permutation prefix; leakage-controlled)
Out-of-Fold (K-fold) Target Encoding (encode each fold from the other folds to block label leakage)
Leave-One-Out Target Encoding (exclude current row's label; still subtly biased)
Count/Frequency Encoding (label-free popularity scalar; complements target encoding)
Empirical-Bayes Target Smoothing (Micci-Barreca: shrink rare-category means toward global prior by count-dependent weight)
Entity Embeddings (learned dense categorical embedding tables)
Scalar Encoding Decoupled from Cardinality (any-cardinality categorical -> fixed handful of dense scalar columns)
No heterogeneous-feature fusion (univariate/channel-independent only; no categorical/static/missing path)
AvgWins is a count-of-best-cells metric, NOT mean error (narrow leads inflate the headline)
High-cardinality fixed-8-dim embedding (RealMLP: constant dim, no frequency awareness, untested on thousands of categories)
Route mismatch: filed under feature-interaction-selection but models NO explicit feature interaction (diagonal scaling is per-feature reweighting only)
Target-encoding label leakage (greedy full-data target stats leak the row's own label; OOF/LOO/ordered construction is mandatory)
A decoder-only foundation model for time-series forecasting (TimesFM)
rtdl — Research on Tabular Deep Learning (FT-Transformer, PLE/Periodic/PLR reference library)
TabPFN-2.5: Advancing the State of the Art in Tabular Foundation Models
Continuous patch-as-token via RevIN-scale + small MLP residual embed (ordinality-preserving, MSE in value space)
TimesFM is univariate-only (no covariates/categoricals/static); Electricity/Traffic/Weather/M4 are IN training, so not zero-shot there
Linformer low-rank key/value compression (sub-quadratic feature-axis attention)
Feature-cell grouping (bundle adjacent feature cells, group size 3) to compress feature-axis sequence
Group adjacent feature-cells to extend a permutation-invariant feature tokenizer to many more columns at ~fixed attention cost
Learned thinking rows appended to in-context set as extra-compute / attention-sink slots
Test-time augmentation over feature transforms (robust/quantile/standard scaling, soft clipping) + appended SVD pseudo-features
TabPFN-2.5 distillation-to-MLP/tree engine is proprietary/unreleased; weights under non-commercial license
TabPFN-2.5 is a vendor technical report; the 100% win rate is vs DEFAULT (untuned) XGBoost — the strong claim is the 87% rate / AutoGluon-1.4 match
TabPFN-Wide: Continued Pre-Training for Extreme Feature Counts
Feature-Widening Synthetic Prior (TabPFN-Wide: continuous-widening + redundant-copy augmentation of the synthetic SCM prior to push the per-feature tokenizer past its feature-count ceiling)
Continued Pre-Training for Foundation-Model Adaptation (adapt frozen TabPFNv2 weights to a new feature-count regime without architecture/tokenizer change)
HDLSS-scoped prior: feature-widening assumes high-dim-low-sample-size with high feature correlation; unproven on many-row, low-correlation, mixed temporal/static wide tables
Prior-engineering (data-side) to extend a per-feature tokenizer's feature ceiling: widen the synthetic pretraining prior + continued pre-training, no tokenizer/architecture change
Feature-aware Modulation for Learning from Temporal Tabular Data
FiLM Feature-wise Linear Modulation (hypernetwork emits per-feature affine gamma/beta conditioning)
Yeo-Johnson Power Transform (signed-value power transform for skewed/heavy-tailed numerics; here time-conditioned)
Time-Conditioned Per-Feature Modulation (shared temporal embedding psi(t) drives a per-feature Yeo-Johnson + affine input-level transform; FiLM-for-tables)
Time-conditioned per-feature affine+power reparameterization: a cheap drop-in input-level transform that aligns each feature to a shared temporal embedding before any tokenizer
Time-conditioned input modulation conflicts with PLR/periodic numerical embeddings (both reshape the numeric input distribution; gains do not stack and may cancel)
Tabular Transformers for Modeling Multivariate Time Series (TabBERT / TabGPT / TabFormer)
Per-Field Quantization to Local Vocabulary (every column discretized into its own finite vocabulary, then learned per-bin embedding)
Hierarchical Field-then-Sequence Transformer (field-transformer compresses N field-tokens to one row embedding, then a sequence-transformer over rows)
Masked Field Modeling (BERT-style MLM over quantized field tokens; TabBERT pretraining)
Uniform quantized-token interface for mixed fields: discretize numeric + categorical fields alike into per-field vocabularies so a single transformer ingests heterogeneous types uniformly

Row-compression before the temporal model: collapse a row's N field-tokens to one row embedding (field-transformer) so the sequence model sees rows, not cells -> controls sequence length on wide tables
Per-Feature Token Stream (each feature emitted as its own token in a flat sequence; the granularity TabFormer's row-compression is an alternative to)
Per-field quantization discards numerical order: bin-id embeddings are unordered lookups, losing magnitude/monotonicity (the weakness PLR/AutoDis fix)
TabFormer frozen-feature gains shown only vs raw features (no GBDT / FT-Transformer / numerical-embedding bar)
TabFormer validated only at 11-12 fields on synthetic fraud data; field-then-sequence hierarchy untested at 70+ heterogeneous fields
TabFlex: Scaling Tabular Learning to Millions with Linear Attention
Learned Semantic-ID Codebooks via RQ-Kmeans (OneRec lineage)
LimiX: A Large Tabular Foundation Model
Non-causal linear attention (phi=elu+1) for order-invariant tabular ICL
Sample-as-token in-context learning (one row = one token)
Softmax self-attention
Mamba state-space sequence model
RQ-Kmeans: residual quantization with k-means centroid codebooks
Discriminative Feature Encoding (low-rank additive column-identity code)
Context-Conditional Masked Modeling (masked joint-distribution objective)
Two-way feature-axis + sample-axis attention
K-means-on-residuals as a training-free codebook for high-cardinality entities
TabFlex 'thousands of features' is a random-projection ceiling, not faithful feature tokenization
Codebook utilization/entropy is a tokenizer-internal metric, not downstream accuracy
RQ-Kmeans quantizer sees one pre-fused embedding, never raw heterogeneous features
LimiX benchmarks TabPFN-v2/TabICL(v1), not TabPFN-2.5/TabICLv2 head-to-head
